{"idea": {"Name": "spectral_precision_routing", "Title": "Spectral Precision Routing: Training-Free Geometric Diagnostics for Dynamic Mixed-Precision Inference", "Short Hypothesis": "The spectral decay rate, covariance condition number, and Hessian curvature of layer-wise activation subspaces directly quantify a layer's sensitivity to quantization-induced perturbations. By dynamically allocating lower precision to geometrically stable layers (low condition number, rapid SVD decay, flat Hessian spectra) and preserving full precision in fragile layers (high condition number, heavy-tailed spectrum, sharp curvature), we can achieve strict memory-bandwidth and latency reductions with theoretically bounded accuracy degradation.
